summary: Backend API tests for engagement milestones feature PASS (23/23). Frontend UI has the code for engagement milestones section but it's not rendering - the /check-engagement-milestones API is sometimes not being called from frontend.

ui_bugs.issue: Engagement Milestones section not rendering in UI despite code existing and API returning data correctly

integration_issues.issue: The /check-engagement-milestones API call appears to not be executing consistently from fetchCardStatus() function. Rare and Epic card endpoints are called but engagement milestones is sometimes skipped.

action_items: Debug why fetchCardStatus() in shop.tsx isn't calling /check-engagement-milestones API - the fetch may be failing silently
Add error handling/logging to the engagement milestones fetch in fetchCardStatus()
Verify the engagementStatus state is being set correctly and triggering a re-render
critical_code_review_comments: Backend implementation for engagement milestones is complete and working correctly
GET /api/users/{user_id}/check-engagement-milestones returns correct data with 3 milestones
Frontend shop.tsx has the Engagement Milestones section code at lines 478-584 but it's not rendering

success_rate.frontend: Partial - Core shop UI works but Engagement Milestones section not visible

seed_data_creation: Test users created with TEST_ prefix for backend testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend APIs for engagement milestones are fully functional. Frontend has the UI code but it's not rendering. Focus debugging on: 1) Whether fetchCardStatus() is reaching the engagement milestones fetch, 2) Whether engagementStatus state is being set, 3) Whether the conditional render engagementStatus.length > 0 is evaluating correctly.
rca_of_the_issue: The engagement milestones backend is complete. Frontend code exists in shop.tsx for the Engagement Milestones section (lines 478-584). The issue appears to be that the fetch sequence in fetchCardStatus() may not be reaching the /check-engagement-milestones API call, or the state update isn't triggering a re-render. The section only renders when engagementStatus.length > 0 but this condition may not be met due to the fetch not completing.
